fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

top_risks: Most evaluation errors are mismatches (not missing outputs)
Over-clustering / many-to-many matching risk detected in correspondences
Severe attribute-level fusion errors detected (very low accuracy)

findings.title: Most evaluation errors are mismatches (not missing outputs)
findings.details: Mismatch ratio is 98.0% among debug reasons. This suggests fusion is producing values, but often incorrect—pointing to wrong mapping, over-matching (wrong records fused), or normalization issues.
findings.recommendation: Sample mismatched cases and categorize: (1) wrong entity cluster (matching error), (2) wrong attribute mapping, (3) formatting/normalization differences. Apply targeted fixes per category and re-run evaluation.

findings.title: Over-clustering / many-to-many matching risk detected in correspondences
findings.details: At least 1 correspondence file(s) are flagged unhealthy by cluster distribution checks. Worst file: correspondences_discogs_lastfm.csv with max_cluster_size=19, large_cluster_ratio=0.0212, one_to_many_ratio=0.1568. This pattern typically indicates over-matching (low thresholds) or blocking collisions, causing many-to-many clusters.

worst_unhealthy_file.diagnosis: The cluster distribution is not long-tail and indicates potential many-to-many matches or over-clustering.

parameters.reason: Increase match thresholds to reduce over-matching and large clusters.

cluster_analysis_overall.diagnosis: One or more correspondence files show a non-long-tail distribution.

parameters.reason: Increase match thresholds to reduce over-matching and large clusters.

findings.recommendation: Increase match thresholds, add stronger blocking keys, and/or introduce one-to-one constraints where appropriate. Review outlier clusters (largest sizes) and tune rules to prevent hub records.

findings.title: Severe attribute-level fusion errors detected (very low accuracy)
findings.details: One or more attributes have very low accuracy; worst is tracks_track_name_accuracy=0.0% over count=23. This typically indicates schema mismatch, wrong attribute mapping, tokenization/normalization issues, or incorrect list/object handling.
findings.recommendation: Inspect the worst-performing attribute end-to-end: source extraction, schema mapping, matching features (if attribute used), and fusion operator. Add normalization (case, punctuation, whitespace), and ensure the correct attribute is being fused (e.g., track name vs release name).

findings.title: Coverage gap: low direct ID coverage against gold/test set
findings.details: Direct coverage ratio is 65.2%. This suggests many gold entities cannot be directly aligned to fused output IDs without extra mapping, often caused by inconsistent identifiers across sources or missing/unstable fused IDs.
findings.recommendation: Standardize entity identifiers early and propagate stable IDs through matching and fusion. Emit an explicit mapping table from source IDs to fused IDs, and ensure evaluation uses that mapping.

findings.title: Fusion size estimate drift (expected vs actual) is non-trivial
findings.details: Worst comparison 'blocking' has rows_pct_error=15.6% (expected_rows=27390, actual_rows=31671, abs_error=4281). This suggests drift between estimated and realized entity counts—often due to conservative blocking/matching estimates, unexpected singleton retention, or changes in match precision/recall.

worst_comparison.name: blocking
worst_comparison.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

findings.recommendation: Recalibrate the size estimator on recent runs: separate estimates for matched-only vs singleton-aware, and validate assumptions about match rate and singleton retention. If drift is increasing, review blocking recall and matching thresholds.

findings.title: Possible noisy list fusion (multi-valued strings) in fused output
findings.details: One or more object columns frequently contain separator-delimited values (e.g., ';', ',', '|', '/'), with high token counts. This can indicate list fusion where conflicting source values were concatenated instead of resolved, reducing data quality.
findings.recommendation: For list-like attributes, prefer set/majority voting with normalization and deduplication. For single-valued attributes, avoid concatenation; select by source trust, recency, or confidence, and add provenance tracking.

recommendations: Sample mismatched cases and categorize: (1) wrong entity cluster (matching error), (2) wrong attribute mapping, (3) formatting/normalization differences. Apply targeted fixes per category and re-run evaluation.
Increase match thresholds, add stronger blocking keys, and/or introduce one-to-one constraints where appropriate. Review outlier clusters (largest sizes) and tune rules to prevent hub records.

Inspect the worst-performing attribute end-to-end: source extraction, schema mapping, matching features (if attribute used), and fusion operator. Add normalization (case, punctuation, whitespace), and ensure the correct attribute is being fused (e.g., track name vs release name).
Standardize entity identifiers early and propagate stable IDs through matching and fusion. Emit an explicit mapping table from source IDs to fused IDs, and ensure evaluation uses that mapping.

Recalibrate the size estimator on recent runs: separate estimates for matched-only vs singleton-aware, and validate assumptions about match rate and singleton retention. If drift is increasing, review blocking recall and matching thresholds.

For list-like attributes, prefer set/majority voting with normalization and deduplication. For single-valued attributes, avoid concatenation; select by source trust, recency, or confidence, and add provenance tracking.
Add a small regression test suite of hand-checked entities to detect mapping changes and over-matching early.
Log per-attribute fusion operator decisions (selected value + source/provenance) to speed up mismatch triage.
Track cluster size distribution over time; alert on increases in max cluster size or one-to-many ratios.

cluster_analysis_unhealthy.diagnosis: The cluster distribution is not long-tail and indicates potential many-to-many matches or over-clustering.

parameters.reason: Increase match thresholds to reduce over-matching and large clusters.

cluster_analysis_overall.diagnosis: One or more correspondence files show a non-long-tail distribution.

parameters.reason: Increase match thresholds to reduce over-matching and large clusters.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

size_estimate_worst_comparison.name: blocking
size_estimate_worst_comparison.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

:

Examples
Register without parameters (exact signature):
>>> strategy.add_evaluation_function(“title”, tokenized_match)
Register with parameters using keyword args (will be bound via partial):
>>> strategy.add_evaluation_function(“director_name”, tokenized_match, threshold=0.7)

id_column
(
Optional
[

) – Identifier column name(s). If a string, the same column is used for
all datasets. If a dict, it should map dataset name -> ID column
for that dataset. When provided, each dataset is normalized to have
a string
_id
column derived from the specified column. If not
provided, the engine will use an existing
_id
column if present,
otherwise fall back to
id
.
include_singletons
(
bool
,
default True
) – Whether to include singleton groups (records not referenced in any
correspondence) in the fused output. Set to False to return only
records that participate in at least one correspondence (group size > 1).
Returns
:
The fused dataset. When
return_provenance
is True and the engine
runs in debug mode, returns a tuple of (dataset, provenance map).
